title: Myself
sentences: My name is Alex.
I am an English learner.
I live in my hometown.
I want to speak English confidently.
I am starting my 30 day speaking journey.
speakingTask: Introduce yourself for 2 minutes. Talk about your name, where you live, what you do, and why you are learning English.

title: Daily Routine
sentences: I wake up at seven in the morning.
I brush my teeth and wash my face.
I have breakfast with my family.
I start my work or study after breakfast.
I relax in the evening and go to bed before midnight.
speakingTask: Describe your daily routine from morning to night for 2 minutes.

title: My Work or Study
sentences: I work or study from Monday to Friday.
I use English in some parts of my work or study.
I talk with colleagues, classmates, or teachers during the day.
I try to stay organized and focused on my tasks.
I want to improve my communication skills for my career.
speakingTask: Talk about your work or studies, your tasks, and why English is important for you.

title: Feelings and Emotions
sentences: Today I feel motivated to practice English.
Sometimes I feel nervous when I speak in front of others.
I feel proud when I finish a speaking task.
I feel relaxed when I practice in a quiet place.
I want to feel confident every time I speak English.
speakingTask: Explain how you feel about speaking English and what makes you feel confident or nervous.

title: Time and Schedule
sentences: I plan my English practice at a fixed time every day.
I spend at least fifteen minutes speaking in English.
I balance my study, work, and rest during the day.
I know that small daily practice creates big results.
I am making English practice a part of my schedule.
speakingTask: Talk about your weekly schedule and when you can practice speaking English.

title: Past Experiences
sentences: I started learning English a long time ago.
I have tried different ways to improve my English.
Sometimes I stopped practicing and lost confidence.
I remember a time when I spoke English very well.
I am ready to learn from my past and move forward.
speakingTask: Share a past experience with English. Talk about a time you were proud or disappointed.

title: Future Plans
sentences: I want to speak English fluently in the future.
I plan to use English for travel or work abroad.
I will keep practicing even after these 30 days.
I will look for opportunities to speak with real people.
I believe my future with English will be bright.
speakingTask: Describe your future plans with English and how speaking skills will help you.

title: Questions and Answers
sentences: What do you do in your free time?
How often do you practice English speaking?
Why do you want to improve your speaking skills?
Who inspires you to learn English?
Where do you usually study or practice English?
speakingTask: Answer these questions in detail, as if you are in an interview.

title: Combination Practice
sentences: I can talk about myself, my routine, and my work.
I can describe my feelings and plans in English.
I can answer simple questions about my life.
I notice that daily speaking practice helps my confidence.
I am ready to combine everything I learned so far.
speakingTask: Give a 3 minute talk that combines who you are, what you do, and your plans with English.

title: First Review
sentences: I have practiced speaking for ten days.
I can already feel some improvement in my speaking.
I know my common mistakes and weak points.
I understand that consistency is more important than perfection.
I am ready to continue to the next level.
speakingTask: Review the first ten days. Talk about what you improved and what you still want to improve.

title: Job Details
sentences: I can clearly describe my main responsibilities at work or school.
I work with different people and need to communicate clearly.
I sometimes need to explain my ideas in English.
I want to sound professional when I talk about my job.
I am learning useful phrases to talk about my work in detail.
speakingTask: Describe your job or studies in detail, including your tasks, tools, and the people you work with.

title: Problems and Solutions
sentences: I sometimes face problems during my day.
I try to stay calm and think about solutions.
I can explain what went wrong in simple English.
I can suggest at least one possible solution.
I know that explaining problems clearly is a useful skill.
speakingTask: Talk about a problem you had recently and explain how you solved it or what you learned.

title: Learning and Growth
sentences: I am always trying to learn something new.
I learn from books, videos, and real life situations.
I make mistakes, but I use them to grow.
I know that language learning is a long journey.
I am proud of every small step I make in English.
speakingTask: Explain how you like to learn new things and how you are learning English right now.

title: Reasons and Explanations
sentences: I can give reasons for my opinions.
I can explain why I made a decision.
I use words like because, so, and therefore.
I try to speak in clear and complete sentences.
I know that good explanations make conversations easier.
speakingTask: Choose three decisions in your life and explain why you made each one.

title: Opinions and Preferences
sentences: I can share my opinions politely.
I can say what I like and what I do not like.
I respect that other people may think differently.
I use phrases like in my opinion and I believe.
I am practicing to express myself with confidence.
speakingTask: Talk about your opinions on work, study, and free time. Explain why you think this way.

title: Comparisons
sentences: I can compare two people, places, or things.
I use words like bigger, more comfortable, or more interesting.
I can say which option I prefer and why.
I can compare my past and present English level.
I understand that comparisons make my speech richer.
speakingTask: Compare your life now with your life five years ago. Talk about work, routine, and English.

title: Storytelling
sentences: I can tell a story with a beginning, middle, and end.
I use time words like first, then, and finally.
I add details to make my story interesting.
I try to speak smoothly without stopping too much.
I know that storytelling is a powerful speaking skill.
speakingTask: Tell a short story about a meaningful day in your life. Focus on clear order and details.

title: Confidence Practice
sentences: I am more confident than when I started.
I can keep speaking even when I make mistakes.
I focus on communication, not perfection.
I know that my voice and ideas are valuable.
I am building a strong speaking habit every day.
speakingTask: Speak freely for three minutes about anything you want. Do not stop, just keep talking.

title: Review and Strengthening
sentences: I have practiced for almost twenty days.
I can feel changes in my speaking and thinking.
I remember many sentences from the earlier days.
I can speak longer without looking at notes.
I am ready to review and strengthen my weak areas.
speakingTask: Review the last ten days. Talk about the topics that were easiest and hardest for you.

title: Intermediate Review
sentences: I have practiced speaking consistently for twenty days.
I am more comfortable talking about my job and daily life.
I can share my opinions and tell short stories.
I understand that my speaking is improving step by step.
I am ready to enter the advanced stage of this plan.
speakingTask: Give a 4 minute talk summarizing your progress so far and your goals for the last ten days.

title: Goals and Vision
sentences: I have clear goals for my English speaking.
I can describe my ideal future using English.
I can explain how English will help my dreams.
I am taking daily action toward my goals.
I see myself speaking English naturally and confidently.
speakingTask: Describe your long term goals in life and explain how English supports each of them.

title: Skills and Strengths
sentences: I can talk about my main skills and strengths.
I can give examples of how I use my skills.
I can explain what makes me a good colleague or friend.
I know that talking about strengths is important in interviews.
I am learning to speak about myself with confidence but without arrogance.
speakingTask: Talk as if you are in an interview and explain your three main strengths with examples.

title: Plans and Projects
sentences: I often plan small personal projects for my life.
I can describe a project from the beginning to the end.
I can say what resources and time I will need.
I can explain possible problems and how to handle them.
I use English to think more clearly about my plans.
speakingTask: Describe a project you want to do this year. Explain the steps, time, and result.

title: Explaining Ideas Simply
sentences: I can explain a difficult idea in simple English.
I avoid long and confusing sentences.
I check if my explanation is easy to understand.
I know that simple language can still be powerful.
I practice explaining ideas to an imaginary friend.
speakingTask: Choose a topic you know well and explain it as if you are teaching a beginner.

title: Teaching and Guiding
sentences: I can give clear instructions in English.
I can guide someone through a simple task.
I use step by step language like first, next, and finally.
I speak slowly and clearly when I give instructions.
I am practicing to be a calm and helpful speaker.
speakingTask: Explain how to do something you know well, like cooking a dish or using a tool.

title: Free Speaking
sentences: I can speak freely for several minutes.
I do not panic when I forget a word.
I can use simple words to explain my meaning.
I can move from one idea to the next smoothly.
I enjoy the feeling of expressing myself in English.
speakingTask: Speak freely for five minutes on any topic. Keep going even if you pause or repeat.

title: AI Speaking Simulation
sentences: I can have a simple conversation with an AI partner.
I answer questions as if I am talking to a real person.
I listen to the questions carefully before I speak.
I try to give full and natural answers.
I use this safe space to experiment with new phrases.
speakingTask: Imagine an AI tutor is asking you questions about your day, plans, and goals. Answer as naturally as possible.

title: Interview Simulation
sentences: I can introduce myself in a professional way.
I can answer common interview questions.
I can talk about my strengths and weaknesses honestly.
I can explain why I am a good fit for a role.
I am practicing to stay calm and confident in interviews.
speakingTask: Simulate a job or school interview. Answer typical questions for at least five minutes.

title: Long Speaking Challenge
sentences: I am ready to speak for a longer time.
I can organize my ideas into clear sections.
I use examples and stories to support my points.
I try to keep a natural and steady rhythm.
I know this challenge will push my speaking ability.
speakingTask: Give a 7 minute talk about your life story, your challenges, and your dreams for the future.

title: Final Reflection
sentences: I have completed the 30 day speaking journey.
I can feel a real change in my confidence.
I understand my speaking strengths and weaknesses.
I know how to continue practicing after this program.
I am proud of my effort and progress in English.
speakingTask: Reflect on the whole 30 days. Talk about your progress, your favorite tasks, and your next steps.
